title: Observability: replace eprintln with tracing everywhere
description:
**Problem**
Mixed use of `tracing` and `eprintln!`. For a "daemon for swarms", logs matter.

**Design**
- Use `tracing` everywhere (esp sync start/stop, retries, backoff, durations, remote id)
- Add structured fields for key events
- Consider counters (sync failures, queue depth, refresh latency) - even structured logs help before full prometheus

**Acceptance**
- [ ] No `eprintln!` in non-test code
- [ ] Sync events have structured tracing spans
- [ ] Key metrics visible in logs (durations, retry counts)

**Files:** grep for eprintln, likely src/daemon/*.rs, src/git/*.rs
darin@book
title: darin@book
Detect and surface remote force-push/divergence
description:
**Problem**
If the remote history is rewritten (force-push), sync currently treats it like a normal remote update and proceeds. There's no explicit detection or warning to the user.

**Design**
Detect non-ancestor relationships between local/remote refs and surface a structured warning/error (e.g., status payload includes `diverged=true`). Consider requiring manual `bd sync --force` for history rewrites.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Divergence detection is explicit
- [ ] User-visible warning surfaced (status/IPC)
- [ ] Tests cover force-push/divergence scenario

**Files:** src/git/sync.rs, src/daemon/core.rs, src/api/mod.rs
darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
Installer hardening: checksum verification + rate-limit robustness
description:
## What's Wrong
Install script fetches `releases/latest` unauthenticated and downloads a tarball without any integrity verification (checksum/signature). It may also fail due to GitHub API rate limits.

## Where
- scripts/install.sh: uses https://api.github.com/.../releases/latest and grep/sed parsing
- scripts/install.sh: downloads release asset and untars with no checksum

## Why It Matters
- Security: users execute a network script piping to bash; we should verify what we download.
- Reliability: rate limiting or JSON format changes can break installs.

## Suggested Fix
- Publish checksums (e.g., SHA256SUMS) as release assets and verify before install.
- Prefer GitHub release page redirect for latest tag, or allow `BD_VERSION` override.
- Use `jq` when available; fallback to robust parsing.

## Acceptance
- Installer verifies integrity of downloaded binary.
- Installer supports explicit version pinning.

title: Release scripts: make version bump portable across macOS/Linux
description:
## What's Wrong
`just release-patch` / `just release-minor` use `sed -i` in a way that's GNU-sed specific; BSD sed (macOS outside nix) requires an extension argument.

## Where
- justfile:67-68, 87-88

## Why It Matters
Contributors running release scripts on macOS without nix will hit a broken release flow.

## Suggested Fix
- Use a portable approach: `perl -pi -e`, or detect BSD vs GNU sed.
- Consider moving release logic to `scripts/release.sh` with explicit checks.

## Acceptance
- Release scripts work on macOS + Linux without requiring GNU sed.
darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
Fix clippy warnings blocking phase 1
description: Clippy fails with too-many-arguments in store_meta::new and daemon/git helpers, plus redundant closure and needless borrow. Clean up to keep cargo clippy -D warnings green before Phase 1 continues.
darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
IPC: unit ResponsePayload variants serialize as null (ambiguous)
description:
**Problem**
`ResponsePayload` is `#[serde(untagged)]` and includes multiple unit variants (`Synced`, `Initialized`, `Pong`, `ShuttingDown`). These serialize to JSON `null`, so clients can deserialize ambiguously.

Example symptom: `bd init` prints `synced` instead of `initialized`.

**Design**
Switch `ResponsePayload` to an explicit tagged representation (e.g. `#[serde(tag="type", content="data", rename_all="snake_case")]`), or ensure unit variants serialize as distinct strings/objects. Keep backward compatibility via versioning or dual-decode.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] `bd init` prints `initialized` on success
- [ ] Unit ok payloads are not serialized as `null`
- [ ] JSON decoding is deterministic
- [ ] Tests pass

**Files:** `src/daemon/ipc.rs`, `src/cli/render.rs`
darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
Fix phase 1 test harness compile errors
description: Enable uuid v5 support for store-id test derivation and wrap BD_DATA_DIR env var mutations in unsafe blocks to satisfy Rust 2024 safety checks.
darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
complete_sync must not drop local mutations on join failure
description:
**Problem**
In `Daemon::complete_sync`, if mutations happened during sync and `CanonicalState::join` fails, the code discards local state and keeps `synced_state`. That can drop mutations made during the sync window.

**Design**
When join fails, detect/resolution collisions (reuse `git::collision` logic) and retry the merge. If merge still fails, keep local state, mark dirty, and surface/log the failure so operators can intervene.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Local mutations are never dropped on join failure
- [ ] Collision resolution is applied for dirty-after-sync merges
- [ ] Tests cover the join-failure path
- [ ] Tests pass

**Files:** src/daemon/core.rs, src/git/collision.rs
darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
Add kill -9 crash recovery integration test for WAL
description:
Add integration test that:
1. Starts daemon
2. Creates beads
3. SIGKILL daemon (before sync completes)
4. Restarts daemon
5. Verifies beads recovered from WAL

Requires subprocess spawning and signal handling in test harness. See tests/critical_path.rs for existing integration test patterns.
darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
Expose sync retry backoff (next retry time) in daemon/CLI status

description: darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
Type-safe daemon: eliminate invariant-checking unwraps
description:
Eliminate unwraps in daemon code by encoding proven invariants in the type system.

**Goal:** No `unwrap()` in non-test daemon paths where failures can be caused by repo state or git IO.

**Approach:** "Parse, dont validate" — move checks to where data enters the system and let types remember what has been validated.

**Subtasks:**
- Serde boundary validation for identity types
- Encapsulate DepKey (no self-loops)
- LoadedRemote proof type
- require_live helper
- with_mutation transaction helper
- Collapse live/tombstones into BeadEntry
- Remove duplicate key from DepEdge

See individual beads for design details.
closed_reason: Core goal achieved: eliminated all .unwrap() calls on bead lookups in daemon paths. Completed: serde boundary validation, DepKey encapsulation, LoadedRemote proof type, require_live helpers + refactoring, with_mutation helper. Remaining low-priority cleanup (bd-mgf, bd-di1) filed as separate beads.

description: darin@book
title: darin@book
Update stateright models for REALTIME_PLAN v0.5
description:
**Problem**
Stateright models in `beads_stateright_models` still reflect v0.4 realtime spec (self-hashing envelopes, unknown-key preservation, GC marker events, PENDING/COMMITTED idempotency). REALTIME_PLAN.md v0.5 simplified hashing, removed unknown-key preservation and GitCheckpointed durability, and deferred GC markers. Models should match v0.5.

**Design**
- Update `beads_stateright_models/src/realtime_types_sketch.rs` to v0.5: hash over EventBody bytes, sha/prev in headers, no unknown-key preservation, no NamespaceGcMarker, no GitCheckpointed.
- Update toy hashing helpers (`beads_stateright_models/src/toy_codec.rs`) and models (`beads_stateright_models/examples/hash_prev_machine.rs`, `beads_stateright_models/examples/canonical_hash_machine.rs`) to use v0.5 hash/prev semantics.
- Update idempotency model (`beads_stateright_models/examples/idempotency_receipt_machine.rs`) to use stable idempotency mapping (no PENDING/COMMITTED receipts).
- Remove or mark GC floor model as deferred in v0.5.
- Refresh doc comments/README/todo in the models crate to reference v0.5 sections.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] All models compile under v0.5 assumptions (no v0.4-only features).
- [ ] Hash/prev models verify sha over EventBody bytes and use header prev_sha semantics.
- [ ] Idempotency model reflects stable request mapping (no pending/committed states).
- [ ] GC floor example is removed or explicitly marked deferred for v0.5.
- [ ] `beads_stateright_models` docs mention v0.5 plan alignment.
- [ ] Tests (model examples) still run.

**Files:**
- beads_stateright_models/src/realtime_types_sketch.rs
- beads_stateright_models/src/toy_codec.rs
- beads_stateright_models/examples/hash_prev_machine.rs
- beads_stateright_models/examples/canonical_hash_machine.rs
- beads_stateright_models/examples/idempotency_receipt_machine.rs
- beads_stateright_models/examples/gc_floor_machine.rs
- beads_stateright_models/README.md
- beads_stateright_models/todo.md
darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Note ID collisions should be guarded
description:
**Problem**
Note IDs are random 8-char base58; collisions are unlikely but possible. A collision overwrites an existing note in the CRDT map.

**Design**
Include actor + timestamp in note IDs or retry generation until unique in-bead. Add a test that forces collision handling.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Note ID generation is collision-safe
- [ ] Tests cover collision path

**Files:** src/core/identity.rs, src/daemon/executor.rs
darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
Add bd dep cycles detection command
description:
beads-go has 'bd dep cycles' to detect circular dependencies in the dependency graph.

Files: src/cli/mod.rs, src/cli/commands/dep.rs

title: Remote URL canonicalization + type tightening
description:
**Problem**
Remote URL normalization is too loose (ssh/https variants, ports, users). This can split canonical state across clones and cause data divergence.

**Design**
Add a stricter RemoteUrl parser that:
- strips scheme/user, lowercases host
- normalizes ssh scp-style and ssh:// URLs
- preserves port when present (host:port)
- trims .git and trailing slashes
Add focused tests for common variants (ssh/https/ssh://, with/without user, with port).

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Same remote in different URL forms maps to identical RemoteUrl
- [ ] Tests cover common cases + edge cases
- [ ] No regressions for file:// or local paths

**Files:** src/daemon/remote.rs
darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
Sync fetch must not clobber local ref (ancestry check + backup)
description:
**Problem**
`SyncProcess::fetch` force-updates `refs/heads/beads/store` to match remote and uses `unwrap_or(Oid::zero())` for ref lookup. If local is ahead/diverged, this overwrites local commits; if ref lookup errors, we treat it as missing. Both can lose durable local history.

**Design**
- Use ref lookup that distinguishes NotFound vs error.
- Only fast-forward local ref when it is an ancestor of remote.
- If local is ahead/diverged, create a backup ref (e.g., `refs/beads/backup/<oid>`) before any update and avoid forcing local to remote.
- Keep commit parent as remote head for linear history.
- Add tests covering local-ahead and diverged scenarios.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] No forced local ref update when local ahead/diverged
- [ ] Backup refs created for diverged local history
- [ ] Ref lookup errors are surfaced
- [ ] Tests cover ancestry check + backup behavior
- [ ] Tests pass

**Files:** src/git/sync.rs, src/git/error.rs
darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
Migration: make imported comment NoteIds globally unique
description:
## What's Wrong
Go comment notes are imported with `NoteId::new(c.id.to_string())`. If comment IDs are not globally unique across issues in beads-go exports, notes can collide and overwrite/merge unexpectedly.

## Where
- src/migrate/go_export.rs:288 (NoteId::new(c.id.to_string()))

## Why It Matters
Data loss / silent merging during migration: two different comments could end up with the same note ID.

## Suggested Fix
- Make note IDs include the issue ID (and/or created_at) e.g. `go-comment-<issue-id>-<comment-id>`.
- Add a migration test fixture with repeated comment IDs across different issues to verify uniqueness.

## Acceptance
- Import never drops/merges distinct comments due to note ID collisions.
closed_reason: Made imported comment NoteIds globally unique by including issue ID in the format: go-comment-{issue_id}-{comment_id}. Added tests to verify uniqueness across issues.

description: darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
Realtime v0.5 implementation
description: Implement REALTIME_SPEC_DRAFT v0.5 per REALTIME_PLAN.md. Track phases 1-7 (REALTIME_PLAN.md §19) and their dependencies.

title: Phase 1: Core identity types + StoreMeta
description:
**Problem**
The realtime lane needs a canonical identity basis; core currently only models ActorId/BeadId/NoteId and still keys correctness by RemoteUrl. Without StoreId/ReplicaId/TxnId/ClientRequestId/StoreEpoch/SegmentId and StoreIdentity, we cannot define stable event identity, receipts, or WAL indexing.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md 0.1 (StoreId as correctness identity), 2.1 (identity types), 15.1 (store meta file)
- Stateright model: beads_stateright_models/src/realtime_types_sketch.rs (section 1 Identity types, StoreIdentity)

**Files:** src/core/identity.rs, src/core/store_meta.rs (new), src/core/mod.rs, src/lib.rs, Cargo.toml
design:
**Design**
- Add newtypes: StoreId, StoreEpoch, ReplicaId, TxnId, ClientRequestId, SegmentId using uuid::Uuid and serde.
- Add StoreIdentity { store_id, store_epoch } as a separate type so the store pair is not duplicated or passed as raw fields.
- Add StoreMeta in src/core/store_meta.rs with fields from the plan: store_id, store_epoch, replica_id, store_format_version, wal_format_version, checkpoint_format_version, replication_protocol_version, index_schema_version, created_at_ms.
- Expose constructors and parsing helpers, plus serde roundtrip tests for each newtype and StoreMeta.
- Re-export new types via src/core/mod.rs and src/lib.rs, and keep ActorId/BeadId/NoteId unchanged.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] New identity types compile and serialize/deserialize (unit tests).
- [ ] StoreMeta matches plan fields and roundtrips via serde.
- [ ] StoreIdentity is used where a pair is required (no duplicated store_id/store_epoch fields).
- [ ] cargo check passes.

description: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Phase 3: SQLite WAL index + WalIndex traits
description:
**Problem**
Phase 3: add WAL SQLite index with schema and reader and writer traits. There is no SQLite index today; WAL is a snapshot file. The realtime plan requires a rebuildable index for event lookup, idempotency, and watermarks.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §0.11 (schema decisions) and §6.2 (table layout)
- SQLite PRAGMA choices in §6.4
**Files:** src/daemon/wal/index.rs (new), src/daemon/wal/mod.rs, Cargo.toml (rusqlite)
design:
**Design**
- Implement schema for events, watermarks, client_requests, origin_seq, segments, meta (per plan).
- Add WalIndexWriter and WalIndexReader traits with methods from REALTIME_PLAN.md §6.1.
- Use UUIDs as 16 byte BLOBs. Store event_ids as CBOR encoded Vec<EventId> in client_requests.
- Configure SQLite with PRAGMA: journal_mode=WAL, synchronous=NORMAL (configurable), foreign_keys=ON, busy_timeout.
- Add schema version in meta table and enforce upgrade rules.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Database initializes with expected tables and indices.
- [ ] WalIndexWriter and WalIndexReader trait methods are implemented.
- [ ] Schema version is stored and validated on open.

description: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Phase 3: WAL replay + index rebuild
description:
**Problem**
Phase 3: implement WAL replay and SQLite index rebuild from WAL segments, including tail truncation rules. Without this, crash recovery, idempotency, and origin_seq allocation are unsafe.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §6.3 (index rebuild and catch up), §6.5 (crash consistency), §7.3 step 8 (replay)
**Files:** src/daemon/wal/replay.rs (new), src/daemon/wal/mod.rs, src/daemon/store_runtime.rs
design:
**Design**
- Implement scan of wal/<namespace>/segment-*.wal in creation order.
- Validate segment headers (store_id, store_epoch, namespace, wal_format_version) before replay.
- For each segment, read frames from last_indexed_offset (if present) to EOF and index records.
- On tail corruption, truncate the segment to the last valid frame boundary and update segments table.
- Provide full rebuild path when index is missing or schema mismatch.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Rebuild populates events, watermarks, and origin_seq tables from WAL segments.
- [ ] Tail truncation is handled safely during replay.
- [ ] Startup can catch up from last_indexed_offset without full scan.

description: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Phase 4: MutationEngine planning + request hash
description:
**Problem**
Phase 4: implement a MutationEngine that turns IPC ops into a single EventBody with canonical request hash and stamps. Current mutations in `src/daemon/executor.rs` mutate state directly; there is no event planning or idempotency digest.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §8.1 and §8.2 (mutation planning and request_sha256)
- request_sha256 storage in WAL headers (REALTIME_PLAN.md §0.11)
**Files:** src/daemon/mutation_engine.rs (new), src/daemon/ops.rs, src/daemon/clock.rs
design:
**Design**
- Add a MutationEngine that validates inputs, enforces limits (MAX_OPS_PER_TXN, MAX_NOTE_BYTES), and builds one EventBody (TxnV1).
- Compute canonical request_sha256 from a normalized request that excludes retry only fields (wait_timeout_ms, durability wait targets).
- Mint txn_id and stamps using HLC; ensure event_time_ms equals stamp wall time and populate hlc_max.
- Return EventDraft { event_body, request_sha256, client_request_id } for WAL append.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] MutationEngine builds a deterministic EventBody draft plus request_sha256 from a canonicalized request.
- [ ] Limit enforcement rejects oversize requests before WAL append.
- [ ] MutationEngine produces plan output without mutating CanonicalState.

description: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Phase 4: Daemon events first mutation pipeline
description:
**Problem**
Phase 4: integrate the events first mutation pipeline in the daemon. Current `src/daemon/executor.rs` mutates CanonicalState directly and writes snapshot WAL. We need WAL append plus deterministic apply_event and watermark updates.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §8.3 (apply pipeline) and §6.5 (crash consistency ordering)
- StoreRuntime integration in §7.1
**Files:** src/daemon/executor.rs, src/daemon/core.rs, src/daemon/store_runtime.rs, src/daemon/wal/mod.rs, src/daemon/query.rs
design:
**Design**
- Replace direct mutation calls with: MutationEngine -> EventWal append -> core::apply_event -> update indexes and watermarks -> schedule replication and checkpoint.
- Enforce idempotency via WAL index lookup before creating a new event when client_request_id is present.
- Persist HLC state and idempotency mapping in the same WAL append critical section.
- Ensure responses are returned only after apply and watermark updates complete.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] All mutation handlers use the events-first pipeline (plan -> WAL append -> apply_event).
- [ ] Idempotent retry returns prior txn_id/event_ids without writing a new event.
- [ ] Watermarks advance only after apply_event completes.

description: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Phase 4: IPC requests + receipts (namespace, durability, client_request_id)
description:
**Problem**
Phase 4: update IPC response shapes for receipts and error payloads. Current `src/daemon/ipc.rs` does not return DurabilityReceipt, and ErrorPayload does not match REALTIME_ERRORS.md.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §0.14 (IPC protocol evolution), §16.1 (response fields and error payload), §16.3 (CLI flags)

**Files:** src/daemon/ipc.rs, src/api/mod.rs, src/cli/mod.rs, src/cli/commands/*, REALTIME_ERRORS.md

**Design**
- Add DurabilityReceipt to success responses and to timeout errors when waiting for replicated durability.
- Replace ErrorPayload shape to `{ code, message, retryable, retry_after_ms?, details?, receipt? }` and keep error codes aligned with REALTIME_ERRORS.md.
- Ensure API JSON types expose receipts without lossy view structs.
- Request field additions and CLI flag plumbing live in bd-3m5.51.
design:
**Design**
- Add optional `namespace`, `durability`, and `client_request_id` fields to mutation requests with defaults (namespace=core, durability=LocalFsync).
- Add DurabilityReceipt to success responses and to timeout errors when waiting for replicated durability.
- Replace ErrorPayload shape to `{ code, message, retryable, retry_after_ms?, details?, receipt? }` and keep error codes aligned with REALTIME_ERRORS.md.
- Plumb CLI flags `--namespace`, `--durability`, `--client-request-id` to IPC requests.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] IPC mutation success responses include DurabilityReceipt in JSON mode.
- [ ] Durability timeouts return ErrorPayload with retryable=true and receipt populated.
- [ ] ErrorPayload schema matches REALTIME_ERRORS.md (code/message/retryable/details/receipt).
- [ ] API JSON types expose receipts without lossy view structs.

description: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Phase 5: Replication protocol types + framing
description:
**Problem**
Replication needs a typed, verifiable on wire protocol. Today there are no CBOR frame types or verification state; without a typestate pipeline, callers can accidentally skip hash or prev link checks and still treat events as valid.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §9.1-9.6 (frame definitions and rules), §0.6 (hashing over EventBody bytes)
- REALTIME_ERRORS.md (error payload shape)
- Stateright model: beads_stateright_models/src/realtime_types_sketch.rs (EventBytes, EventFrameV1, VerifiedEventAny)

**Files:** src/daemon/repl/frame.rs (new), src/daemon/repl/proto.rs (new), src/daemon/repl/mod.rs (new), src/core/event.rs
design:
**Design**
- Define CBOR envelope `{ v, type, body }` and per-message bodies (HELLO, WELCOME, EVENTS, ACK, WANT, ERROR) with spec field names.
- Introduce EventFrameV1 that carries EventId, sha256, prev_sha256, and EventBytes<Opaque>.
- Add verification pipeline types: VerifiedEvent<PrevVerified> vs VerifiedEvent<PrevDeferred> (or equivalent) so callers cannot treat a deferred prev check as fully verified.
- Enforce MAX_FRAME_BYTES, MAX_EVENT_BATCH_BYTES, and decoding limits before allocating large buffers; reject oversize frames with ERROR(code="frame_too_large").
- Keep ErrorPayload consistent with IPC; ERROR frames use the same schema and codes.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Encode/decode roundtrip tests for each replication message type.
- [ ] VerifiedEvent typestate prevents forwarding events with deferred prev checks.
- [ ] Oversized frames and batches are rejected deterministically with correct error codes.
- [ ] Error frames serialize to the same schema as IPC ErrorPayload.

description: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Phase 5: EventBroadcaster + hot cache
description:
**Problem**
Phase 5: add a bounded EventBroadcaster and hot cache for recently appended events. There is no pub or sub mechanism to stream events or serve fast replication reads.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §7.5 (EventBroadcaster and hot cache requirements)
**Files:** src/daemon/broadcast.rs (new) or src/daemon/store_runtime.rs
design:
**Design**
- Implement a broadcaster that publishes EventId, sha, prev_sha, namespace, and canonical EventBody bytes.
- Enforce bounds by events and bytes; drop slow subscribers with ERROR(code="subscriber_lagged").
- Maintain a FIFO hot cache with deterministic eviction.
- Integrate publish after WAL append in StoreRuntime.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Broadcaster delivers events in order to subscribers and drops slow consumers when bounds exceeded.
- [ ] Hot cache evicts by size and event count deterministically.
- [ ] API exposes publish/subscribe with bounded channels and a drop reason.
- [ ] Unit tests cover backpressure and eviction behavior.

description: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Phase 5: Replication runtime integration
description:
**Problem**
This bead is now the integration glue for replication runtime. Sessions/manager/server are split into separate beads; we need to wire them into the daemon coordinator with bounded channels, correct defaults, and backpressure so replication traffic is serialized safely.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md sections 7.5, 9.1-9.8, 7.2 (store runtime wiring)
- Split beads: bd-3m5.46 (session), bd-3m5.47 (manager), bd-3m5.48 (server)
**Files:** src/daemon/repl/mod.rs, src/daemon/core.rs, src/daemon/run.rs
design:
**Design**
- Add a repl module root to share wiring/config between session/manager/server.
- On daemon startup, spawn replication server + manager threads and register bounded channels into the coordinator.
- Route inbound replication ingest through the coordinator (WAL append + apply) and produce ACKs via session channel.
- Route outbound fanout from EventBroadcaster (with WAL fallback) and enforce AdmissionController limits at ingress.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Daemon startup wires replication server + manager and registers channels to the coordinator.
- [ ] Inbound replication events reach the coordinator through bounded queues and produce ACKs.
- [ ] Outbound sessions receive events via broadcaster or WAL range fallback.
- [ ] Overload path rejects replication ingest (no unbounded buffering).

description: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Phase 5: DurabilityCoordinator + peer ACK tracking
description:
**Problem**
Phase 5: implement DurabilityCoordinator for LocalFsync and ReplicatedFsync(k). There is no coordinator to track peer ACKs or advance durable watermarks beyond local fsync.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §10 (durability coordination), §0.12 (applied vs durable watermarks), §2.1.2 (replica roster)
**Files:** src/daemon/durability_coordinator.rs (new), src/daemon/store_runtime.rs, src/daemon/repl/*
design:
**Design**
- Track pending txns by txn_id and required durability class.
- For ReplicatedFsync(k), select eligible replicas (from roster if present) and wait for durable ACKs.
- On success, return updated receipt with durability_proof and advance durable watermarks.
- On insufficient eligible replicas, return ERROR(code="durability_unavailable").
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] ReplicatedFsync(k) completes when k eligible replicas ACK.
- [ ] Insufficient eligible replicas triggers immediate error.
- [ ] Durable watermark advances only after local fsync and required ACKs.

description: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Phase 6: Checkpoint canonical JSON + manifest/meta structs
description:
**Problem**
Phase 6: implement canonical JSON helper and checkpoint manifest and meta structs. There is no checkpoint lane module yet, so manifest and meta definitions do not exist.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §13.3-13.5 (canonical JSON, manifest.json, meta.json)
**Files:** src/git/checkpoint/json_canon.rs (new), src/git/checkpoint/manifest.rs (new), src/git/checkpoint/meta.rs (new), src/git/checkpoint/mod.rs (new)
design:
**Design**
- Add `to_canon_json_bytes<T: Serialize>(v: &T) -> Vec<u8>` with sorted key ordering and no extra whitespace.
- Define Manifest and Meta structs with fields from plan; implement content_hash and manifest_hash calculation.
- Add tests verifying canonical JSON stability and hash determinism.
acceptance_criteria:

title: Phase 1: Namespaces + StoreState
description:
**Problem**
The core state is currently single namespace. Namespace boundaries are implicit ("core") and there is no NamespaceId or namespace policy type, which makes cross namespace bugs easy and prevents typestate around per namespace invariants.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md 0.3 (StoreState choice), 0.4 (namespace policy representation), 2.2 (Namespaces)
- Stateright model: beads_stateright_models/src/realtime_types_sketch.rs (section 3 Namespaces)

**Files:** src/core/namespace.rs (new), src/core/store_state.rs (new) or src/core/state.rs, src/core/mod.rs, src/lib.rs, src/config.rs (policy parsing hook)
design:
**Design**
- Add NamespaceId newtype with validation [a-z][a-z0-9_]{0,31}, matching the stateright model; include as_str and display.
- Add NamespacePolicy and CheckpointGroup structs with the fields and defaults from REALTIME_PLAN.md 2.2.
- Introduce StoreState as BTreeMap<NamespaceId, CanonicalState> with helpers: get, get_mut, ensure_namespace, and a constant default NamespaceId::core().
- Ensure callers must select a namespace before accessing a CanonicalState (no implicit global operations), so cross namespace operations require explicit opt in.
- Re-export NamespaceId, NamespacePolicy, CheckpointGroup, StoreState in src/core/mod.rs and src/lib.rs.
acceptance_criteria:

- [ ] StoreState can create and return the default core namespace without side effects.

title: Phase 7: IPC Subscribe streaming + require_min_seen
description:
**Problem**

- Use EventBroadcaster for live updates and WAL read for historical backfill.

acceptance_criteria:

title: Phase 1: Core error types + ErrorPayload
description:
**Problem**
We need a single, truthful error vocabulary shared by IPC and replication. Today error mapping is scattered (OpError/CoreError/IpcError) and ErrorPayload in src/daemon/ipc.rs lacks retryable/receipt fields. That creates multiple representations of the same failure and forces clients to interpret strings ad hoc.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §0.14 (error payload standardization) and §9.10 (replication error handling)
- REALTIME_ERRORS.md (canonical error code registry)
- Current error plumbing: src/core/error.rs, src/error.rs, src/daemon/ipc.rs, src/daemon/repl/proto.rs (new)

**Files:** src/core/error.rs (new or expanded), src/error.rs, src/daemon/ipc.rs, src/api/mod.rs, src/daemon/repl/proto.rs, REALTIME_ERRORS.md
design:
**Design**
- Introduce ErrorCode enum with stable string mappings for every code in REALTIME_ERRORS.md; make it the single source of truth for code strings.
- Define ErrorPayload { code, message, retryable, retry_after_ms?, details?, receipt? } in core and re-use it in IPC JSON and replication CBOR.
- Provide From conversions from CoreError/OpError/IpcError/SyncError into ErrorPayload with consistent retryable/effect semantics.
- Enforce that unknown codes are tolerated by clients; only retry when retryable=true.
- Keep ErrorPayload schema version stable; add fields only as optional to preserve compatibility.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] ErrorCode covers every entry in REALTIME_ERRORS.md and is the sole mapping to code strings.
- [ ] ErrorPayload serializes to both JSON (IPC) and CBOR (replication) with optional fields omitted when empty.
- [ ] Tests verify retryable and receipt fields are preserved and unknown codes do not break decoding.

description: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Phase 4: HLC persistence + recovery
description:
**Problem**
HLC state must be persisted to prevent stamp regression after restart or wall-clock rollback. Recovery must reconstruct HLC from WAL if SQLite is missing.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §2.8 (write stamps explicit requirements)
- §6.2 (hlc table in SQLite schema)

**Design**
- Add `hlc` table to SQLite schema: (actor_id TEXT PK, last_physical_ms INTEGER, last_logical INTEGER)
- On startup: initialize HLC from max(persisted, system_time)
- On mint: clamp forward jumps to HLC_MAX_FORWARD_DRIFT_MS (default 600s), log anomaly
- On index rebuild: scan WAL records for hlc_max and take max per ActorId
- Persist HLC in same critical section as WAL append/index commit

**Acceptance**
- [ ] HLC state persisted in SQLite hlc table
- [ ] Restart recovers HLC without stamp regression
- [ ] Forward jump clamping logged as anomaly
- [ ] Index rebuild reconstructs HLC from WAL hlc_max

**Files:** src/daemon/wal/index.rs, src/core/time.rs, src/daemon/mutation_engine.rs
darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Phase 3: WatermarkHeadMap + head sha tracking
description:
**Problem**
Head sha tracking is required to validate prev_sha continuity and to safely advance watermarks. Today we only have seq values, which allows the system to represent seq>0 without knowing the chain head, making continuity checks ambiguous. This bead scopes the Phase 3 core tracking (no replication session wiring).

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md 0.12 (head sha rule) + 2.3 (WatermarkHeadMap)
- Stateright model: beads_stateright_models/src/realtime_types_sketch.rs (HeadStatus)

**Design**
- Extend watermarks to carry head hash for each (namespace, origin) pair (HeadStatus: Genesis, Known, Unknown).
- Require head to be Known whenever seq>0; Unknown is allowed only when a checkpoint does not include heads.
- Persist head sha in the wal.sqlite watermarks table (applied_head_sha, durable_head_sha) and keep in memory in StoreRuntime.
- Update head sha on WAL append (durable) and after apply_event (applied) so prev_sha validation can use the latest known head.
- Provide helper API: advance_contiguous(seq, head) and observe_at_least(seq, head) to enforce invariants in one place.
- Replication session wiring deferred to Phase 5 (bd-3m5.46).

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Head sha is persisted and reloaded alongside applied and durable watermarks.
- [ ] API rejects advancing a nonzero seq without a head sha.
- [ ] StoreRuntime exposes head sha lookup per (namespace, origin) for later prev_sha validation.
- [ ] Head sha updates are tied to watermark advancement (no stale head).

**Files:** src/core/watermark.rs, src/daemon/store_runtime.rs, src/daemon/wal/index.rs
design:
**Design**
- Extend watermarks to carry head hash for each (namespace, origin) pair (HeadStatus: Genesis, Known, Unknown).

- Provide helper API: advance_contiguous(seq, head) and observe_at_least(seq, head) to enforce invariants in one place.

description: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Phase 5: GapBufferByNsOrigin for replication
description:
**Problem**
Out-of-order replication needs a bounded gap buffer that is aware of contiguity and prev hash verification. A naive BTreeMap of bytes lets callers accidentally treat deferred prev checks as safe and doesn’t encode the decision outcomes.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §9.4 (contiguous ingest rule), §9.8 (gap handling + bounds)
- Stateright model: beads_stateright_models/src/realtime_types_sketch.rs (GapBuffer, VerifiedEventAny, IngestDecision)

**Files:** src/daemon/repl/gap_buffer.rs (new), src/daemon/repl/session.rs, src/daemon/store_runtime.rs
design:
**Design**
- Implement GapBufferByNsOrigin keyed by (NamespaceId, ReplicaId) with per-origin gap state.
- Store VerifiedEventAny so deferred prev checks remain explicit; only contiguous VerifiedEvent<PrevVerified> are forwarded to WAL append.
- Provide ingest API returning an IngestDecision: ForwardContiguousBatch, BufferedNeedWant, DuplicateNoop, Reject(code).
- Enforce per-origin bounds on buffered events and bytes, plus a timeout window; overflow or timeout returns Reject("gap_buffer_overflow"/"gap_timeout").
- Drain buffered suffix in order when the predecessor arrives; stop if a deferred event is still waiting on prev verification.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Gap buffer enforces event and byte bounds and returns explicit Reject on overflow/timeout.
- [ ] Only fully verified contiguous events are forwarded to WAL append.
- [ ] Duplicate events are treated as no-ops without disturbing the buffer.
- [ ] Unit tests cover: single gap, multiple gaps, overflow, timeout, deferred-prev behavior.

description: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Phase 5: AdmissionController + overload shedding
description:
**Problem**
Plan requires priority ordering and overload shedding to protect local mutations from replication storms.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §0.19 (overload shedding, internal backpressure defaults)
- Priority: local IPC > replication ingest > WANT servicing > checkpoint

**Design**
- Add `AdmissionController` to src/daemon/admission.rs
- Track inflight counts: ipc_mutations, repl_ingest_bytes, repl_ingest_events
- Enforce limits: MAX_IPC_INFLIGHT_MUTATIONS (1024), MAX_REPL_INGEST_QUEUE_BYTES (32 MiB), MAX_REPL_INGEST_QUEUE_EVENTS (50k)
- When overloaded: shed replication first, then IPC
- Return ERROR(code="overloaded") with retry_after_ms

**Acceptance**
- [ ] AdmissionController tracks inflight by priority class
- [ ] Replication shed before IPC mutations
- [ ] Limits enforced with ERROR(code="overloaded")
- [ ] Tests verify shedding order

**Files:** src/daemon/admission.rs (new), src/daemon/core.rs, src/daemon/repl/session.rs
darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Phase 1: Watermarks + durability types
description:
**Problem**
Realtime semantics require explicit applied vs durable watermarks and head hash tracking. Today there is no watermark type, no seq0 vs seq1 distinction, and receipts cannot represent pending or partial durability without ambiguity.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md 0.12 (applied vs durable), 0.12 head sha rule, 10.1 (durability classes), 16.1 (receipt shape)
- Stateright model: beads_stateright_models/src/realtime_types_sketch.rs (Watermarks, Watermark, HeadStatus, DurabilityClass, DurabilityOutcome)

**Files:** src/core/watermark.rs (new), src/core/durability.rs (new), src/core/mod.rs, src/lib.rs
design:
**Design**
- Add Seq0 and Seq1 newtypes: Seq0 for highest contiguous seen, Seq1 for actual event origin_seq (1 based). Provide helpers next(), prev(), prev_seq0().
- Add Watermark<K> that couples Seq0 with HeadStatus (Genesis, Known(sha), Unknown). This prevents representing seq>0 with no head.
- Add Watermarks<Applied> and Watermarks<Durable> maps keyed by (NamespaceId, ReplicaId) with helpers advance_contiguous and observe_at_least that enforce head requirements.
- Add DurabilityClass with ReplicatedFsync using NonZeroU32 for k; add DurabilityOutcome (Achieved vs Pending) and DurabilityProofV1 to express receipts without ambiguous Option fields.
- Keep types serde friendly for IPC and WAL metadata.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] WatermarkMap and WatermarkHeadMap helpers enforce contiguous advance and head sha required for seq > 0.
- [ ] Seq0/Seq1 helpers behave as expected and are used by Watermark APIs.
- [ ] DurabilityClass cannot represent k=0; DurabilityOutcome encodes pending vs achieved explicitly.
- [ ] serde roundtrip tests for WatermarkMap/WatermarkHeadMap and DurabilityClass/Outcome.

description: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Phase 6: CheckpointSnapshot barrier type
description:
**Problem**
Checkpoint export requires a snapshot barrier that freezes the state and included watermarks together. Without a typed barrier, the coordinator can accidentally mix shards from different logical times or claim watermarks that aren’t represented in the exported content.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §13.8 (checkpoint snapshot barrier and consistency rule)
- Checkpoint scheduling in §13.7 and export workflow in §13.8

**Files:** src/git/checkpoint/types.rs (new), src/git/checkpoint/export.rs, src/daemon/store_runtime.rs
design:
**Design**
- Introduce an immutable CheckpointSnapshot struct: { group_id, policy_hash, roster_hash, included_watermarks (durable), included_heads?, shard_payloads/dirty_shards, created_at_ms }.
- The coordinator thread captures the snapshot under a single lock so shard contents and watermarks correspond to the same logical point in time.
- Make the snapshot owned/immutable (e.g., Arc or owned values), so the worker thread cannot observe concurrent mutations.
- Enforce a check: included_watermarks must not exceed the max seq represented by shard_payloads; if it would, delay snapshot or recompute.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Snapshot captures included watermarks and shard contents from a single coordinator tick.
- [ ] Snapshot object is immutable once created and safe to pass to worker thread.
- [ ] Export worker consumes only snapshot data (no live reads during I/O).
- [ ] Snapshot includes metadata needed for manifest/meta construction without touching live state.

description: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Phase 7: admin.status + admin.metrics IPC ops
description:
**Problem**
Operators need introspection into store health, watermarks, segments, replication, and checkpoints.

**Context**

**Design**

- admin.metrics returns: counters (wal_append_ok/err, apply_ok/err, repl_events_in/out), gauges (IPC inflight, repl queue depth, per-peer lag), histograms (wal_append_duration, checkpoint_duration)
- Both return structured JSON

**Acceptance**
- [ ] admin.status returns complete store introspection
- [ ] admin.metrics returns counters/gauges/histograms
- [ ] CLI `bd admin status` and `bd admin metrics` work
- [ ] Tests verify expected fields

title: Phase 7: admin.doctor + admin.scrub_now IPC ops
description:
**Problem**
Operators need bounded health checks with deterministic remediation actions.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §16.1 (admin.doctor, admin.scrub_now)

**Design**
- admin.doctor: runs bounded health scrub, returns machine-readable JSON with:
  - checks: [{ id, status, severity, evidence, suggested_actions }]
  - summary: { risk, safe_to_accept_writes, safe_to_prune_wal, safe_to_rebuild_index }
  - Must include explicit remediation actions (exact admin ops to run)
- admin.scrub_now: samples N events per namespace (default 200), verifies WAL CRC32c and sha256, verifies SQLite offsets, optionally verifies checkpoint_cache
  - Returns structured pass/warn/fail results

**Acceptance**
- [ ] admin.doctor returns structured checks with remediation
- [ ] admin.scrub_now samples and verifies bounded records
- [ ] CLI `bd admin doctor` and `bd admin scrub` work

title: Phase 7: admin.maintenance_mode + admin.rebuild_index
description:
**Problem**
Operators need to put daemon in read-only mode for repairs and rebuild SQLite from WAL.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §16.1 (admin.maintenance_mode, admin.rebuild_index)

**Design**
- admin.maintenance_mode: toggle read-only mode
  - When enabled: reject mutations/replication ingest with ERROR(code="maintenance_mode")
  - Allow admin/read operations
  - Required for online repair beyond tail truncation
- admin.rebuild_index: rebuilds SQLite index from WAL segments
  - Explicit operator action; requires maintenance_mode
  - Scans all WAL segments and re-indexes events

**Acceptance**
- [ ] admin.maintenance_mode toggles write rejection
- [ ] admin.rebuild_index scans WAL and rebuilds SQLite
- [ ] CLI `bd admin maintenance on/off` and `bd admin rebuild-index` work

title: Phase 7: admin.fingerprint for divergence detection
description:
**Problem**
Operators need to quickly detect divergence across replicas using stable convergence fingerprints.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §16.1 (admin.fingerprint, fingerprint algorithm)

**Design**
- Returns per-namespace: { state_sha256, tombstones_sha256, deps_sha256, namespace_root }
- Merkle-ish algorithm: shard_hash[00..ff] -> type_root -> namespace_root
- Modes: full (per-shard hashes, expensive), sample(N) (deterministic N samples)
- Sampling uses sha256(namespace || request_nonce) to select indices
- Includes watermarks_applied and watermarks_durable as basis

**Acceptance**
- [ ] admin.fingerprint returns namespace_root and type_roots
- [ ] full mode returns per-shard hashes
- [ ] sample(N) returns deterministic shard samples
- [ ] Tests verify determinism (same state -> same hash)

- admin.rotate_replica_id: generates new replica_id, updates meta.json, logs old->new mapping
  - Used for recovery from duplicated store directories
  - New replica starts with origin_seq=0 for new identity

**Acceptance**

title: Phase 3: bd store unlock CLI for stale lock recovery

**Problem**
Operators need to recover from stale lock files without manual rm.

title: Phase 6: Legacy Git ref migration import
description:
**Problem**
Existing users have data in refs/heads/beads/store. Must import into new checkpoint format.

design:

title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Phase 5: PeerAckTable for durability coordination
description:
**Problem**
ReplicatedFsync(k) requires a truthful view of which peers have durably ACKed which watermarks and head hashes. A naive map risks mixing namespaces or ignoring head hash divergence.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §0.12 (applied vs durable + head sha), §10.1-10.3 (durability coordination), §9.5 (ACK semantics)
- Stateright model: beads_stateright_models/src/realtime_types_sketch.rs (Watermarks<Durable>, HeadStatus)

**Files:** src/daemon/repl/peer_acks.rs (new), src/daemon/durability_coordinator.rs, src/daemon/store_runtime.rs
design:
**Design**
- PeerAckTable tracks per peer: durable Watermarks<Durable>, optional durable_heads, and last_ack_at_ms.
- Enforce monotonicity on update: seq may only advance; head sha must match the seq (reject divergence).
- Provide queries: acked_by(ns, origin, seq) -> Vec<ReplicaId>, and satisfied_k(ns, origin, seq, k) -> bool.
- Maintain per-namespace eligibility via roster (durability_eligible); only count eligible peers.
- Surface divergence: if a peer reports the same seq but a different head sha, mark that peer as diverged and exclude from durability quorum.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] PeerAckTable stores per-peer durable/applied watermarks and heads with monotonic updates.
- [ ] Divergent head sha at same seq is detected and excluded from eligibility.
- [ ] satisfied_k computes k-of-n for eligible peers with explicit failure cases.
- [ ] Unit tests cover monotonic updates and satisfied_k behavior.

title: Phase 2: EventBody canonical CBOR + hashing
description:
**Problem**
Realtime WAL and replication require a canonical EventBody representation whose bytes are the hash preimage. There is no EventBody type or canonical encoding path in core yet, so we cannot guarantee stable hashing or verify remote frames.

design:

title: Phase 4: IPC namespace + durability + client_request_id fields
description:
**Problem**
IPC currently has no namespace, durability class, or client_request_id fields, and defaults are implemented ad hoc in CLI/daemon. This makes retries and read-your-writes semantics ambiguous and invites mismatched defaults across entry points.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §0.14 (IPC evolution) and §16.1 (request fields and defaults)

design:
**Design**
- Extend mutation request structs with optional fields: namespace, durability, client_request_id, actor_id, preserving backward compatibility.
- Parse namespace into NamespaceId and client_request_id into ClientRequestId early, so validation happens before mutation planning.
- Centralize defaulting rules in one place (e.g., Request::normalize): namespace defaults to core, durability defaults to LocalFsync, actor_id defaults to daemon actor.
- Add read/query gating fields require_min_seen and wait_timeout_ms; require_min_seen uses applied watermarks and returns retryable timeout error on miss.
- Ensure IPC JSON schema stays stable: new fields are optional and omitted in serialization when None.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Mutation request structs add optional namespace/durability/client_request_id/actor_id with backward-compatible defaults.
- [ ] Read/query request structs add optional require_min_seen + wait_timeout_ms with consistent defaulting.
- [ ] CLI flags --namespace, --durability, --client-request-id map to IPC fields.
- [ ] Defaulting (namespace=core, durability=LocalFsync, actor_id=daemon default) is applied consistently.

**Design**
- Define DurabilityProofV1 to capture the local fsync proof and optional replication acks.
- Define DurabilityOutcome (Achieved vs Pending) to avoid ambiguous Option<DurabilityClass> fields.
- Define DurabilityReceipt { store: StoreIdentity, txn_id, event_ids, durability_proof, outcome, min_seen } with serde support.
- Keep types serde-friendly for IPC JSON; daemon/IPC wiring lives in bd-3m5.14.
design:
**Design**
- Define DurabilityProofV1 to capture the local fsync proof and optional replication acks.
- Define DurabilityOutcome (Achieved vs Pending) to avoid ambiguous Option<DurabilityClass> fields.

title: Phase 2: core apply_event for EventBody
description:
**Problem**
The daemon mutates CanonicalState directly, so apply semantics are scattered and can diverge across mutation and replication paths. We need a single pure apply_event path that deterministically maps EventBody deltas to CRDT joins.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md 0.8 (deterministic apply), 2.4 (TxnDeltaV1), 8.3 (apply pipeline)
- Stateright model: beads_stateright_models/src/realtime_types_sketch.rs (TxnOpV1, TxnDeltaV1)

**Files:** src/core/apply.rs (new), src/core/state.rs, src/core/bead.rs, src/core/dep.rs, src/core/tombstone.rs
design:
**Design**
- Add core::apply_event(&mut CanonicalState, &EventBody) -> ApplyOutcome as a pure function with no I/O.
- Apply TxnDeltaV1 ops in canonical op key order so repeated application is deterministic.
- Use existing CRDT joins (Lww, Tombstone::join, DepEdge::join) and preserve tombstone resurrection rules.
- Detect note id collisions with mismatched content as corruption (return error), and treat duplicate note append as idempotent.
- Return ApplyOutcome with changed bead ids, dep keys, and note ids to drive indexing and checkpoint dirtiness.
acceptance_criteria:

title: IPC: split malformed_payload vs invalid_request for JSON decode
description:
**Problem**
IPC JSON decode errors currently map to legacy parse_error, and semantic request validation failures get mislabeled as parse_error because decode_request_with_limits deserializes directly into Request. REALTIME_PLAN.md/REALTIME_ERRORS.md require malformed_payload for invalid JSON and invalid_request for valid JSON with semantic issues.

**Where**
- src/daemon/ipc.rs: decode_request_with_limits, From<IpcError> for ErrorPayload

**Design**
- Separate JSON parsing from Request validation (e.g., parse to serde_json::Value first), then validate/deserialize with explicit error mapping.
- Map malformed JSON to code malformed_payload with details {parser:"json"...}, and semantic validation to invalid_request.
- Ensure payload shape uses realtime fields {code,message,retryable,retry_after_ms?,details?,receipt?}.

**Acceptance**
- Invalid JSON yields malformed_payload (not parse_error).
- Semantically invalid JSON yields invalid_request.
- IPC error payloads remain realtime-shaped and include details where applicable.

**Files**
- src/daemon/ipc.rs

title: Idempotency: detect client_request_id reuse mismatch
description:
**Problem**
The realtime code client_request_id_reuse_mismatch exists but cannot be emitted. WAL index upsert overwrites existing (namespace, origin_replica_id, client_request_id) rows, hiding mismatched reuse and mutating history.

**Where**
- src/core/error.rs: ErrorCode::ClientRequestIdReuseMismatch (defined)
- src/daemon/ops.rs: OpError lacks a variant
- src/daemon/wal/index.rs: upsert_client_request uses ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE

**Design**
- Add OpError variant and IPC mapping for client_request_id_reuse_mismatch with details {namespace, client_request_id, expected_request_sha256, got_request_sha256}.
- Change index behavior to preserve first-seen mapping; detect mismatch and return explicit error instead of overwriting.
- Keep idempotent reuse path returning original txn/event ids (no mutation) when request_sha matches.

**Acceptance**
- Reusing client_request_id with different request_sha256 returns client_request_id_reuse_mismatch (retryable=false).
- WAL index no longer overwrites request_sha256/txn_id/event_ids on conflict.
- Matching reuse returns prior ids without mutating history.

**Files**
- src/daemon/ops.rs
- src/daemon/wal/index.rs
- src/core/error.rs

title: Idempotent retries reuse WAL index mapping
description:
**Problem**
Mutation planning generates a fresh txn_id per retry via the current write stamp. There is no visible fast-path to reuse existing txn/event ids for repeated client_request_id, which breaks idempotent retry semantics in REALTIME_PLAN.md.

**Where**
- src/daemon/mutation_engine.rs: MutationEngine::plan uses txn_id_for_stamp(&store, &write_stamp)
- src/daemon/wal/index.rs: WalIndexReader::lookup_client_request exists but is not used in planning path

**Design**
- Before planning a mutation, lookup client_request_id in the WAL index.
- If found with matching request_sha256, return a plan that reuses the stored txn_id/event_ids and short-circuits event creation.
- If not found, proceed with normal planning and insert mapping.

**Acceptance**
- Retrying the same request returns the same txn/event ids (idempotent).
- No duplicate txn rows for repeated client_request_id.
- Coverage in Phase 4 idempotency tests (bd-3m5.60) updated.

**Files**
- src/daemon/mutation_engine.rs
- src/daemon/wal/index.rs

title: Phase 3: Store dir layout + store lock
description:
**Problem**
Phase 3: implement store directory layout and a store global lock. Current paths only expose a base data dir and WAL uses per remote snapshot filenames. Realtime needs `$BD_DATA_DIR/stores/<store_id>/` with a lock file and secure permissions.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §0.2 (process level locking), §15.1 (store layout), §7.3 (startup flow steps 2-4)
**Files:** src/paths.rs, src/daemon/store_lock.rs (new), src/daemon/store_runtime.rs (new) or src/daemon/core.rs
design:
**Design**
- Add path helpers in `src/paths.rs`: stores_dir, store_dir(store_id), store_meta_path, wal_dir, index_path, checkpoint_cache_dir.
- Implement a store lock file in the store dir with metadata: store_id, replica_id, pid, started_at_ms, daemon_version, last_heartbeat_ms.
- Acquire lock at store open; on failure show metadata and fail fast. No auto delete.
- Enforce file permissions (0700 for dirs, 0600 for sensitive files) and reject symlinks for critical paths.
- Expose a helper API for lock acquire and release (called by StoreRuntime open).
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Store directory helpers return expected paths under BD_DATA_DIR.
- [ ] Lock file is created with required metadata and permissions; failure surfaces metadata.
- [ ] Store open fails if lock held.
- [ ] `cargo check` passes.

description: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
WAL replay: map wal_format_unsupported and header decode vs mismatch
description:
**Problem**
Replay maps all SegmentHeader errors to segment_header_mismatch, including unsupported wal_format_version and header decode failures. REALTIME_ERRORS.md distinguishes wal_format_unsupported from semantic header mismatch and from corruption.

**Where**
- src/daemon/wal/segment.rs: SegmentHeader::decode reports unsupported wal format version
- src/daemon/ops.rs: wal_replay_error_code maps SegmentHeader to segment_header_mismatch
- src/daemon/ipc.rs: mirrored mapping

**Design**
- Add a specific error variant (or reason parsing) to detect wal_format_version mismatch and map to wal_format_unsupported with details {wal_format_version, supported}.
- Split decode failures (bad magic/CRC/truncated header) from semantic mismatch and map to wal_corrupt (or specific corruption code) instead of segment_header_mismatch.

**Acceptance**
- Unsupported wal_format_version yields wal_format_unsupported.
- Header decode failures yield corruption code, not segment_header_mismatch.
- True store_id/store_epoch mismatch continues to use segment_header_mismatch.

**Files**
- src/daemon/wal/segment.rs
- src/daemon/ops.rs
- src/daemon/ipc.rs

title: WAL replay: verify payload sha256 (hash_mismatch)
description:
**Problem**
Replay does not verify sha256(payload_bytes) vs the record header, so hash_mismatch is effectively unreachable. CRC alone does not catch a writer that stored an incorrect sha.

**Where**
- src/daemon/wal/replay.rs: scan_segment decodes record payload but does not hash raw bytes
- src/daemon/wal/record.rs: RecordHeaderMismatch lacks a sha mismatch variant

**Design**
- Preserve raw payload bytes and compare sha256(payload_bytes) to record.header.event_sha256.
- Introduce a RecordHeaderMismatch variant (or WalReplayError) for hash mismatch and map to ErrorCode::HashMismatch with details.

**Acceptance**
- Replay detects sha mismatch and emits hash_mismatch with details.
- CRC-only mismatches continue to map to wal_corrupt.

**Files**
- src/daemon/wal/replay.rs
- src/daemon/wal/record.rs
- src/daemon/ops.rs
- src/daemon/ipc.rs

title: WAL index: detect equivocation on EventId reuse
description:
**Problem**
Inserting an event with the same (namespace, origin_replica_id, origin_seq) but different sha triggers a SQLite constraint error and is mapped to index_corrupt, leaving equivocation unreachable.

**Where**
- src/daemon/wal/index.rs: SqliteWalIndexTxn::record_event inserts into events with PK on (namespace, origin_replica_id, origin_seq)
- src/daemon/ops.rs: wal_index_error_code maps WalIndexError::Sqlite(_) to index_corrupt

**Design**
- On conflict, detect existing row and compare sha256; if different, emit equivocation with details (namespace, origin_replica_id, origin_seq, expected_sha256, got_sha256).
- Avoid using generic SQLite error for equivocation.

**Acceptance**
- Equivocation is reported via ErrorCode::Equivocation, not index_corrupt.
- Normal duplicate insert with same sha remains idempotent (no error).

**Files**
- src/daemon/wal/index.rs
- src/daemon/ops.rs
- src/daemon/ipc.rs

title: WAL replay: surface wal_tail_truncated repairs
description:
**Problem**
Replay silently truncates a corrupted tail and drops ReplayStats; operators never see wal_tail_truncated even though the registry expects it.

**Where**
- src/daemon/wal/replay.rs: scan_segment sets truncated=true and truncates tail
- src/daemon/store_runtime.rs: open(...) discards ReplayStats

**Design**
- Emit wal_tail_truncated (retryable=true) when tail repair occurs, with details {namespace, segment, offset}.
- Surface ReplayStats to callers (StoreRuntime::open) so IPC can report the repair.

**Acceptance**
- Tail truncation emits a realtime error payload with details.
- StoreRuntime open path exposes replay stats for operator visibility.

**Files**
- src/daemon/wal/replay.rs
- src/daemon/store_runtime.rs
- src/daemon/ipc.rs

title: IPC error payloads: include details for WAL/index + lock permission_denied
description:
**Problem**
store_runtime_error_payload drops details for WAL index/replay errors, and lock permission_denied omits path/operation details. REALTIME_ERRORS.md expects structured details for machine handling.

**Where**
- src/daemon/ipc.rs: store_runtime_error_payload (WalIndex/WalReplay)
- src/daemon/ipc.rs: store_lock_error_payload (PermissionDenied without details)

**Design**
- Extend error payload mapping to include details for WAL index/replay errors (e.g., store_epoch_mismatch, prev_sha_mismatch, index_corrupt).
- Include permission_denied details for lock IO ({path, operation}).

**Acceptance**
- WAL/index error payloads include structured details per registry.
- Permission denied on lock IO includes path and operation.

**Files**
- src/daemon/ipc.rs
- src/daemon/ops.rs
- src/core/error.rs (if new details types needed)

title: Retryable flags: align lock_held and index_* with registry
description:
**Problem**
retryable flags do not match REALTIME_ERRORS.md: lock_held is marked retryable but should be false; index_corrupt and index_rebuild_required are treated as permanent but should be retryable after rebuild.

**Where**
- src/daemon/ops.rs: store_runtime_transience (lock_held)
- src/daemon/ops.rs: wal_index_transience (index_corrupt/index_rebuild_required)

**Design**
- Update transience mapping to reflect registry retryable semantics.
- Ensure IPC ErrorPayload.retryable mirrors updated transience values.

**Acceptance**
- lock_held -> retryable=false
- index_corrupt/index_rebuild_required -> retryable=true

**Files**
- src/daemon/ops.rs
- src/daemon/ipc.rs

title: Admission/backpressure: emit overloaded/rate_limited/maintenance_mode
description:
**Problem**
REALTIME_ERRORS.md defines overloaded, rate_limited, and maintenance_mode, but OpError has no variants and IPC cannot emit these codes. Limits exist but no visible error signaling.

**Where**
- src/core/error.rs: ErrorCode::{Overloaded,RateLimited,MaintenanceMode}
- src/daemon/ops.rs: OpError missing variants
- src/core/limits.rs: admission/backpressure limits

**Design**
- Add OpError variants and IPC mappings for overloaded/rate_limited/maintenance_mode with retry_after_ms when available.
- Hook AdmissionController/maintenance mode checks to return these errors on request admission.

**Acceptance**
- Overload and maintenance mode conditions emit the corresponding realtime error codes.
- retry_after_ms is set when known.

**Files**
- src/daemon/ops.rs
- src/daemon/ipc.rs
- src/daemon/* admission path (as implemented in bd-3m5.29)

title: Namespace policy errors: emit namespace_policy_violation/cross_namespace_dependency
description:
**Problem**
ErrorCode::{NamespacePolicyViolation,CrossNamespaceDependency} exist but have no OpError variants or IPC emission path, so namespace policy enforcement cannot surface structured errors.

**Where**
- src/core/error.rs: ErrorCode definitions
- src/daemon/ops.rs: OpError missing variants

**Design**
- Add OpError variants and IPC mappings with structured details per registry.
- Emit these codes from policy enforcement in replication/IPC admission once policies are loaded.

**Acceptance**
- Policy violations produce namespace_policy_violation or cross_namespace_dependency with details.

**Files**
- src/daemon/ops.rs
- src/daemon/ipc.rs
- src/daemon/* policy enforcement paths (post bd-3m5.17, bd-3m5.35)

title: Replication ingress: map DecodeError to realtime codes
description:
**Problem**
DecodeError is rich (non_canonical, decode limits, trailing bytes), but replay collapses decode failures into wal_corrupt and there is no visible mapping for replication error frames. REALTIME_ERRORS.md expects actionable codes (invalid_request, frame_too_large, batch_too_large, non_canonical).

**Where**
- src/core/event.rs: DecodeError
- src/daemon/wal/replay.rs: decode errors -> wal_corrupt
- replication ingress path (Phase 5)

**Design**
- Add DecodeError -> ErrorPayload mapping for replication/IPC ingress.
- Use non_canonical for canonical CBOR violations; size-related errors map to frame_too_large/batch_too_large; other semantic decode errors -> invalid_request.

**Acceptance**
- Replication ERROR frames include realtime codes for decode limits/non_canonical.
- Non-canonical CBOR is not reported as wal_corrupt in network paths.

**Files**
- src/core/event.rs
- src/daemon/* replication ingress code (bd-3m5.17/48)
- src/daemon/ipc.rs (if IPC shares mapping)

title: Phase 3: StoreRuntime + StoreId identity mapping
description:
**Problem**
Phase 3: introduce StoreRuntime and move daemon identity from RemoteUrl to StoreId (hybrid mapping). Today `Daemon` is keyed by `RemoteUrl` and uses `path_to_remote` in `src/daemon/core.rs`, which blocks per store WAL and replication.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §0.1 (StoreId as correctness identity) and §7.1-7.4 (StoreRuntime + discovery order)
- Migration fallback UUIDv5(normalized_remote_url) in §0.16
**Files:** src/daemon/core.rs, src/daemon/remote.rs, src/daemon/repo.rs, src/paths.rs, src/daemon/store_runtime.rs (new)
design:
**Design**
- Add `StoreRuntime` struct (initially minimal fields: meta, policies, state, watermarks, wal handle) in `src/daemon/store_runtime.rs`.
- Change `Daemon` maps to `BTreeMap<StoreId, StoreRuntime>` plus `path_to_store_id` and `remote_to_store_id` caches.
- Implement store identity discovery order: local store meta.json, refs/beads/meta store_meta.json, refs/beads/<store_id> listing, UUIDv5(normalized_remote_url) fallback for migration only.
- Keep RemoteUrl mapping for legacy git sync, but do not key correctness on it.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Daemon state keyed by StoreId; remote URL only used for legacy git lane mapping.
- [ ] Store discovery order matches plan and is covered by tests or structured logs.
- [ ] Existing commands still resolve a store from repo path (compat) without behavior regression.

description: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Phase 3: Event WAL framing + segment writer
description:
**Problem**
Phase 3: implement the event WAL segment format and framing. Current `src/daemon/wal.rs` is a snapshot WAL; there is no event log, framing, or segment rotation.

**Context**
- REALTIME_PLAN.md §0.9 (segment naming), §0.10 (LocalFsync), §6.4 and §6.5 (crash consistency)
- Framing uses crc32c (REALTIME_PLAN.md §9.1)
**Files:** src/daemon/wal/mod.rs (new), src/daemon/wal/frame.rs (new), src/daemon/wal/segment.rs (new), src/daemon/wal/record.rs (new)
design:
**Design**
- Define WAL segment header with store_id, store_epoch, namespace, wal_format_version, created_at_ms, segment_id.
- Define record header with EventId fields, sha256, prev_sha256 (optional), event_time_ms, txn_id, client_request_id, request_sha256 (optional), and record_len.
- Implement frame encoding with length + crc32c and tail truncation handling.
- Implement segment rotation by size and age; create files under wal/<namespace>/segment-<created_at_ms>-<segment_id>.wal.
- Implement LocalFsync semantics: fsync segment after append; fsync directory only on new segment creation.
acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Can append and read back a record frame with crc32c validation.
- [ ] Segment creation and rotation follow naming and fsync rules.
- [ ] MAX_WAL_RECORD_BYTES enforced before writing.

title: darin@book
Tombstone GC policy can allow ID reuse/resurrection
description:
**Problem**
Tombstones are GC'd after 30 days in sync merge. If an ID is resurrected after GC, collisions can reappear or deleted IDs can be reused unintentionally.

**Design**
Revisit GC: either make tombstones permanent, use a much longer TTL, or store a compact ID-reuse guard (e.g., bloom filter or min hash) to prevent resurrection. Consider making GC configurable.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] GC policy documented and configurable or safe by default
- [ ] Regression test for resurrection after GC

darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
Daemon init should not ignore ensure_repo_loaded errors; dedupe SyncWait
description:
## What's Wrong
`Request::Init` ignores errors from `ensure_repo_loaded` (TODO in code). Also `SyncWait` behavior is duplicated: `Daemon::handle_request` returns Synced immediately, but `run_state_loop` has special-case barrier logic.

## Where
- src/daemon/core.rs:585 (TODO: ignores ensure_repo_loaded errors after init)
- src/daemon/core.rs:559 (SyncWait returns immediately)
- src/daemon/server.rs:45 (SyncWait handled as a barrier before calling daemon)

## Why It Matters
Init can report success even if repo state isn't loadable, leading to confusing follow-on failures. Duplicated SyncWait logic makes correctness brittle and creates drift.

## Suggested Fix
- Make Init return an error (or include warning details) if `ensure_repo_loaded` fails.
- Consolidate SyncWait into one place (prefer state loop barrier) and remove/clarify the other path.

## Acceptance

**Problem**

description: darin@dusk
title: darin@dusk
Daemon load blocks on SSH auth, hangs IPC
description:
**Problem**
First access to a repo blocks the daemon in `Daemon::ensure_repo_loaded` while waiting for `GitOp::Load` to finish. `GitWorker::load` always performs a remote fetch (even in best-effort mode). If SSH auth is misconfigured (no agent / passphrase prompt / stalled handshake), libgit2 fetch can hang. Because `ensure_repo_loaded` waits synchronously, the state thread blocks and IPC requests like `bd status` hang indefinitely. We saw `bd status` time out on this machine before SSH auth was configured.

**Design**
Make load non-blocking, and treat timeouts as a guardrail rather than the primary fix:
- Split `GitWorker::load` into a fast local-only load (read local ref, compute state) and a remote refresh path.
- Change `ensure_repo_loaded` to return local state immediately and, if the repo is stale, enqueue a background `GitOp::Refresh` (or `GitOp::Fetch`) without blocking.
- Only block on remote fetch when no local ref exists; add a bounded timeout (default ~30s, configurable) and return a clean, actionable error on timeout with diagnostic info (SSH agent/auth hints, remote URL).
- Surface fetch/auth errors via `fetch_error` and Sync warnings, but keep IPC responsive.

**Design Notes**
The primary goal is responsiveness: read-only queries must not block on network or auth. Use best-effort refresh for freshness, but never let it block IPC. Timeout is a secondary safety net for unavoidable fetches. Keep existing CRDT merge semantics intact.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] `bd status` returns promptly (e.g., <1s) when SSH auth is missing/invalid; no hang.
- [ ] If local ref exists, initial load is non-blocking and refresh happens in the background.
- [ ] If no local ref exists, the initial fetch is bounded by a default ~30s timeout and returns a clean, actionable error on timeout with diagnostics.
- [ ] Fetch/auth failures appear as warnings (`fetch_error`) but do not block IPC responses.
- [ ] Background refresh still updates cached state once auth/network is available.
- [ ] Run `codex review --uncommitted` (timeout 10m) and address findings.
- [ ] Tests pass.

**Files:** `src/daemon/core.rs`, `src/daemon/git_worker.rs`, `src/git/sync.rs`

title: darin@dusk
WAL replay mid-file corruption should fail fast
description:
**Problem**
WAL replay truncates on any corruption, including mid-file CRC/record decode errors, because scan_segment doesn't distinguish tail vs mid-file. This violates REALTIME_PLAN.md 5.2 (online vs offline boundary), which requires fail-fast on mid-file corruption and only tail truncation repairs.

**Design**
In src/daemon/wal/replay.rs scan_segment, detect tail vs mid-file:
- tail: remaining < FRAME_HEADER_LEN, frame_len > remaining, or crc mismatch where offset+frame_len == file_len.
- mid-file: return explicit WalReplayError (new variant) with path+offset; do not truncate.
Only truncate in tail cases when repair_tail=true. Surface operator action to run bd store fsck.
Add tests for mid-file corruption (expect error) and tail corruption (truncate) using WAL fixtures.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Mid-file corruption during replay returns error and does not truncate.
- [ ] Tail corruption still truncates and continues.
- [ ] Tests cover mid-file vs tail behavior; cargo test passes.

**Files:** src/daemon/wal/replay.rs, tests/phase3_wal.rs

**Problem**

**Design**
Make refresh async:
- If repo is clean and refresh TTL hit, kick off background load
- Serve current cached state meanwhile
- Apply refreshed state when it arrives (maybe invalidate caches)
- Optionally: let queries request `fresh=true` when they really need it

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Slow git fetch doesnt block unrelated client requests
- [ ] Stale reads are bounded (TTL still respected eventually)
- [ ] Fresh flag available for queries that need it

CRDT join property tests (commutative/associative/idempotent)
description:
**Problem**
We don’t have property tests proving CRDT join algebra and invariants hold across random states. A subtle regression could cause silent data loss.

**Design**
Add proptest generators for CanonicalState (beads + tombstones + deps) and assert:
- join(a,b) == join(b,a)
- join(a, join(b,c)) == join(join(a,b), c)
- join(a,a) == a
- invariants hold after join (live ∩ tombstones = ∅, dep indexes consistent)
Use minimal valid field generation (safe ActorId/BeadId) and keep sizes small for speed.

title: darin@dusk
Track sealed WAL segments + final_len invariant
description:
**Problem**
wal.sqlite segment rows are always sealed=false and final_len=None; replay does not enforce sealed segment file_len == final_len. REALTIME_PLAN.md 5.2 requires sealed segment invariants and fail-fast on mismatch.

**Design**
When rotating segments, mark the previous segment sealed with final_len (bytes_written). Persist sealed/final_len in wal.sqlite segments table. On startup/replay, if a sealed segment exists and file_len != final_len, return WalReplayError and surface operator action. Add fsck check to flag sealed length mismatch.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Segment rotation updates wal.sqlite segments with sealed=true and final_len.
- [ ] Replay errors on sealed segment length mismatch.
- [ ] Fsck reports sealed length mismatch.
- [ ] Tests cover sealed invariant enforcement; cargo test passes.

**Files:** src/daemon/wal/segment.rs, src/daemon/wal/index.rs, src/daemon/wal/replay.rs, src/daemon/wal/fsck.rs, tests/phase3_index.rs

title: darin@darinsmacstudio.lan
Sync fails when migrating from beads-go in PR branch with existing store
description:
**Problem**
When migrating from beads-go to beads-rs in a PR branch, the sync doesn't work properly. Beads appear not to exist after migration.

**Root Cause**
The `migrate from-go` command bypasses the daemon entirely - it directly writes to `refs/heads/beads/store` using `SyncProcess` (src/cli/commands/migrate.rs:71-74). But the daemon caches state in memory per-remote in `RepoState`. After migration completes:
1. Git ref has the migrated state
2. Daemon still has empty/old cached state
3. CLI queries go through daemon, return stale data

**Design**
Add a `Request::Refresh` IPC command that tells the daemon to reload state from the git ref for a given repo. The migrate command should call this after successful migration.

Alternatively, migrate could go through the daemon IPC entirely, but that's more invasive.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] After `bd migrate from-go`, `bd list` shows the migrated beads
- [ ] Works even if daemon was already running before migration
- [ ] Test: migrate in a fresh clone, verify state is visible

**Files:** src/daemon/ipc.rs, src/cli/commands/migrate.rs, src/daemon/core.rs
closed_reason: Fixed: added Request::Refresh and notify daemon after migration

title: WAL write failure must abort mutation + preserve state
description:
**Problem**
Mutations can succeed even when WAL writes fail. In `src/daemon/core.rs` `mark_dirty_and_schedule` logs a warning and proceeds, so a crash can lose acknowledged data. There is no rollback of in-memory state when WAL writes fail.

**Design**
Introduce a transactional mutation path that applies changes to a cloned `CanonicalState`, writes the WAL entry first, and only commits the new state + dirty flag on success. Add a dedicated OpError (e.g., `WalError`/`DurabilityError`) surfaced via IPC. Ensure WAL sequence only advances on successful write. Add a unit test that forces WAL write failure and verifies no state change + error return.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] WAL write failures return an error and do not modify repo state
- [ ] WAL sequence/dirty flags remain unchanged on WAL failure
- [ ] Successful WAL writes still schedule sync
- [ ] Tests cover WAL failure path
- [ ] Tests pass

description: darin@book
title: darin@dusk
Collapse live/tombstones into single BeadEntry map
description:
**Problem**
The invariant "live ∩ tombstones = ∅" is enforced by careful method design:
```rust
pub struct CanonicalState {
    live: BTreeMap<BeadId, Bead>,
    tombstones: BTreeMap<BeadId, Tombstone>,
    // ...
}
```

This relies on `insert`/`delete` being correct. A bug could violate the invariant.

**Design**
Make the invariant structurally impossible:
```rust
pub enum BeadEntry {
    Live(Bead),
    Tombstone(Tombstone),
}

pub struct CanonicalState {
    beads: BTreeMap<BeadId, BeadEntry>,
    deps: BTreeMap<DepKey, DepEdge>,
}
```

Now a bead cannot be both live and tombstoned - the type system prevents it.

**Design Notes**
- Bigger refactor than the other type improvements
- Consider doing this when touching `state.rs` for other reasons
- `get_live`/`get_tombstone` become pattern matches on entry
- Iteration patterns change (`live.values()` → `beads.values().filter_map(...)`)
- Wire format (`src/git/wire.rs`) may need adjustment

**Acceptance**
- [ ] `BeadEntry` enum in `src/core/state.rs`
- [ ] `CanonicalState` uses single `beads: BTreeMap<BeadId, BeadEntry>`
- [ ] All accessors (`get_live`, `get_tombstone`, iteration) updated
- [ ] Wire format handles the change
- [ ] Invariant comment updated to note its now structural
- [ ] Tests pass

**Files:** src/core/state.rs, src/git/wire.rs

title: Durability gap: ops succeed before anything hits disk
description:
**Problem**
Mutations update in-memory state and schedule a sync later. If the process crashes or power dies before the debounce fires, acknowledged work is lost.

**Design Options**

*Option A:* Local WAL/journal
Append-only ops or periodic snapshot in the socket dir; replay on daemon start.

*Option B:* Immediate local-only git commit  
On each mutation, write a local-only beads commit immediately (durable), push/compact later. Adds commits but can squash in compaction pass.

**Design Notes**
User concern: Option B adds commits. Consider squash-on-push or a separate local ref that gets force-pushed after compaction.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Crash after acknowledged mutation doesnt lose data
- [ ] Recovery mechanism tested (kill -9 during burst, restart, verify state)
- [ ] Performance acceptable (write latency)

**Files:** src/daemon/mod.rs, src/daemon/executor.rs
closed_reason: Implemented WAL-based durability. Replaced local git commits with atomic WAL file writes (~10x faster). Recovery on startup merges WAL with git state. Unit tests pass; full kill -9 integration test deferred.

description:
**Problem**
HLC relies on wall clock time. If a machine clock jumps backward or is far ahead, its stamps can dominate LWW merges and effectively overwrite other actors' updates. This can look like data loss.

**Design**
Persist the last-seen WriteStamp (per-repo) and clamp local wall_ms to >= last_seen.wall_ms. If local time moves backwards, advance logical counter instead of wall time. Add skew detection (warn if now << last_seen or now >> last_seen by threshold). Consider storing max observed stamp in meta or WAL to survive restarts.

**Acceptance**
- [ ] Stamps remain monotonic across restarts and backward clock jumps.
- [ ] Skew detection is surfaced (status/log).
- [ ] Tests simulate backward/forward jumps and verify monotonicity + merge ordering.

**Files:** src/daemon/clock.rs, src/core/time.rs, src/daemon/core.rs

**Files:** src/core/state.rs, src/daemon/query_executor.rs, src/daemon/executor.rs
design:
**In-memory only** — indexes live in `RepoState`, never persisted to deps.jsonl.

**Rebuild on load:** When daemon starts or merges from git, iterate deps once to build:
```rust
struct DepIndexes {
    out_edges: BTreeMap<BeadId, Vec<(BeadId, DepKind)>>,  // from -> [(to, kind)]
    in_edges: BTreeMap<BeadId, Vec<(BeadId, DepKind)>>,   // to -> [(from, kind)]
}
```

**Incremental update:** In `apply_add_dep` / `apply_remove_dep`, push/remove from both maps.

**Functions to change:**
- `deps_from()` (state.rs:300) → lookup `out_edges[id]`
- `deps_to()` (state.rs:309) → lookup `in_edges[id]`  
- `compute_blocked_by()` (query_executor.rs:707) → iterate `in_edges` filtered by kind
- `would_create_cycle()` (executor.rs:1073) → BFS uses `out_edges` directly

Complexity drops from O(all deps) to O(neighbors per hop).

description: darin@book
title: darin@book
session_coordinator_boundary model hits counterexample for pending ingest property

This indicates either the property is too strict for v0.5 semantics (buffered/out-of-order ingest) or the model logic is wrong.
